item.title: Hello, World!
item.description: This is the first post of my new blog. It's mostly for testing and style tweaking, so it's fairly sparse.

item.title: Introducing: chalk-blog
item.description: For the past few months I've started having ideas for blog articles I wanted to write. Not having an actual blog made that somewhat difficult, so naturally, that led into how I could develop my own blogging system...

item.title: Making Node CLI Apps: The Basics
item.description: Node is actually a pretty good choice when you want to make a command line application. On my journey to create chalk-blog, I had to research some of these basics and figured I would consolidate them into a single blog for posterity.

item.title: Unraveling Promises
item.description: A common issue I've seen some developers struggle with is correctly utilizing the Promise chain. Promises give us the ability to reduce callback hell in our code, but without proper understanding, they could create additional complexity instead. This easily leads into spaghetti code that is hard to follow and hard to maintain. Below, I will explain the nuances of the Promise chain using examples of complex code and how we can simplify.

item.title: ezcli
item.description: As mentioned in Introducing: chalk-blog, while working on my blogging system I pulled out various useful pieces into their own packages. These were parts of the system I thought could be useful outside the scope of chalk-blog and thus deserved their own dedicated distributions. ezcli was the first of those packages.
